title: The OKF Standards Landscape: RDF, OWL, SKOS, JSON-LD, PROV-O
concept: OKF is a stack: RDF encodes facts as triples, OWL adds logical inference, SKOS structures vocabularies, JSON-LD is developer-friendly serialization, PROV-O captures provenance. Together they let machines share meaning, not just tokens.
why_it_matters: Vector embeddings alone lose semantic structure. RDF/OWL preserve it so your twin can reason and cite sources — a non-negotiable for consulting credibility.
learn_5min: Read W3C RDF 1.1 Primer sections 1–2, then JSON-LD 1.1 Primer section 1.
apply_10min: Convert 5 facts about yourself into JSON-LD triples using schema.org/Person context; save as me.jsonld.

reference_projects.name: Wikidata
reference_projects.description: Largest live RDF knowledge graph — pattern library for real-world ontology use.

reference_projects.name: DBpedia
reference_projects.description: Wikipedia expressed as structured triples; canonical ontology example.

reference_projects.name: Schema.org
reference_projects.description: Pragmatic web-scale vocabulary; use its Person, Organization, CreativeWork classes.

consulting_angle: Frame OKF as the governance layer for 'AI-ready knowledge' — beyond what vector-DB vendors offer.
twin_platform_hook: OKF = source of truth; vectors and graph indexes are derived. Never let a derived index be canonical.

title: Vector vs. Graph vs. Hybrid Retrieval — Choosing Your Memory Substrate
concept: Vector search = fuzzy semantic similarity. Graph search = explicit multi-hop reasoning. Hybrid = both + BM25 keyword fallback. Real twins are always hybrid.
why_it_matters: A twin must answer multi-hop, identity-grounded questions; pure vector stores can't traverse relationships or explain reasoning paths.
learn_5min: Read Microsoft Research GraphRAG announcement + one Qdrant hybrid-search tutorial.
apply_10min: Build a 3-column table: Query type → Best retriever → Why. Include multi-hop, semantic, and exact-match examples from your own work.

reference_projects.name: Microsoft GraphRAG
reference_projects.description: Reference implementation of graph-augmented retrieval with community summarization.

reference_projects.name: Neo4j GenAI Stack
reference_projects.description: Docker-compose starter combining Neo4j, LLM, and vector search.

reference_projects.name: LlamaIndex PropertyGraphIndex
reference_projects.description: Hybrid graph+vector retrieval in a single abstraction.

resources.title: GraphRAG: Unlocking LLM discovery on narrative private data (Microsoft Research)

consulting_angle: Enterprise RAG plateaus at 60–70% quality when vector-only; selling hybrid architecture is a repeatable engagement pattern.
twin_platform_hook: Canonical graph in Neo4j or Cosmos DB Gremlin; derived vectors in Azure AI Search / Qdrant; BM25 as safety net.

title: Agent Architectures 101: ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, Reflexion, Multi-Agent
concept: Four canonical patterns: ReAct (reason-act-observe), Plan-and-Execute (upfront decomposition), Reflexion (self-critique), Multi-Agent (supervisor/swarm/hierarchy). All modern frameworks are variations of these.
why_it_matters: Wrong pattern choice is the #1 reason agent PoCs fail. Your twin will need a supervisor + specialists topology to separate ingestion, retrieval, reasoning, and tone.
apply_10min: Sketch your twin's agent topology: nodes labeled with pattern + OKF slice each owns.

reference_projects.name: LangGraph examples
reference_projects.description: Reference implementations of ReAct, plan-execute, reflexion, and multi-agent patterns.

reference_projects.name: OpenAI Swarm
reference_projects.description: Minimal, readable multi-agent handoff library — great for learning.

reference_projects.name: Anthropic Cookbook
reference_projects.description: Production-grade agent recipes from Anthropic engineering.

resources.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

consulting_angle: Use Anthropic's 'start simple' framing to deflate over-engineered client demands and land higher-margin scoped engagements.
twin_platform_hook: MVP twin = one ReAct agent with OKF tools. Add supervisor+specialists only after a failure mode is observed twice.

title: Model Context Protocol (MCP): The USB-C for AI Agents
concept: MCP is an open protocol (Anthropic, Nov 2024) standardizing how LLMs access tools, resources, and prompts. Servers expose primitives; any MCP-compatible client can consume them without custom integration.
why_it_matters: Solves the N×M integration problem. Build your OKF as an MCP server once → every agent, IDE, and workflow can query it. Highest-leverage architectural bet for the twin platform.
learn_5min: Read modelcontextprotocol.io Introduction + spec Overview section.
apply_10min: Install the filesystem or GitHub MCP server into Claude Desktop or Cursor; query your own repo in natural language.

reference_projects.name: modelcontextprotocol/servers
reference_projects.description: Official reference MCP servers: filesystem, GitHub, Postgres, Slack, and more.

reference_projects.name: Awesome MCP Servers
reference_projects.description: Community catalog of 100+ MCP servers across domains.

reference_projects.name: Azure MCP Server
reference_projects.description: Microsoft's official MCP server exposing Azure resources to any MCP client.

consulting_angle: MCP strategy is the 2025 version of the 'API strategy' conversation. Lead the client narrative before vendors do.
twin_platform_hook: OKF-MCP Server becomes the twin platform's public interface — reusable across n8n, Codex, Claude, ChatGPT.

title: GraphRAG vs. Classic RAG — Why Graphs Enable Grounded Judgment
concept: Classic RAG retrieves by vector similarity, blind to relationships. GraphRAG extracts entities+relations into a graph, detects communities, generates summaries, and retrieves using structure + vectors — enabling thematic and multi-hop answers.
why_it_matters: The twin's core value is grounded judgment across fragmented sources — precisely what GraphRAG delivers, with defensible provenance paths.
learn_5min: Read the Microsoft GraphRAG 'How it works' section on the GitHub README.
apply_10min: Take 3 past consulting reports; list 10 entities + 15 relationships you'd extract. This becomes your ontology v0.

reference_projects.name: Microsoft GraphRAG
reference_projects.description: Reference GraphRAG implementation with local + global query modes.

reference_projects.name: Neo4j LLM Graph Builder
reference_projects.description: No-code entity extraction from documents into Neo4j.

resources.title: From Local to Global: A GraphRAG Approach (Microsoft)

consulting_angle: GraphRAG is the natural phase-2 upsell after a client's vector-only RAG PoC plateaus.
twin_platform_hook: Ingestion pipeline: content → LLM extraction → merge into OKF graph → embed nodes → cache community summaries for global queries.
